page: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but does not include any GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, scoring in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both small-block and large-block read-only operations reaching top-tier performance in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Passmark CPU scores, and database operations also rank in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests.

The D128lds_v6 offers a high density of compute cores and substantial local storage capacity, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high parallel processing power without massive memory footprints. The 2.0 GB per core memory allocation represents a tradeoff, as memory-intensive applications may encounter bottlenecks despite the high-speed memory bandwidth. This server is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its advanced vector and matrix extensions can be fully utilized.
description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v6 is an x86_64 server featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of memory, providing a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 7559 GB of local storage. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities. However, single-core performance remains average. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require large local storage but do not demand high memory-to-core ratios. It is suited for parallel processing, database management, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference utilizing its integrated AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v6 is a compute-focused x86_64 server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128lds_v6 is a 128-vCPU Intel Xeon server with 256 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute server featuring massive local storage and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
7559 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Low-memory configuration of 256 GB RAM yielding 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based inference
